scenarios.query: Write instructions for TypeScript files: strict mode, no any, named exports only
expected_behavior: All rules are imperative and verifiable — no 'should' or 'prefer'
File length matches concise variant (under 20 lines of rules)

scenarios.description: User provides a vague rule that cannot be applied
scenarios.query: Add a rule: write good, clean code
expected_behavior: Rejects the vague rule as unverifiable
Asks what specific behavior 'good clean code' means
Offers concrete rewrites: function length limit, naming pattern, etc.
Does NOT emit the original vague rule unchanged

scenarios.description: User adds a rule that contradicts an existing workspace instruction
scenarios.query: Add a rule: always use default exports
expected_behavior: Checks or asks about existing instructions that may conflict
Flags contradiction if workspace already says 'use named exports only'
Asks user to confirm which rule should take precedence
Does NOT silently add a contradicting rule
